title: haplopappus racemosus subsp. liatriformis
intro: Haplopappus racemosus subsp. liatriformis, also known as a subspecies of the Haplopappus racemosus plant, is a flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Haplopappus racemosus subsp. liatriformis include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antifungal and antibacterial activity. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or decoction, and its extracts are used in herbal medicine to promote healing and prevent infection.
